pr.title: Fix ftcolanim/ftmaterial split and fix string usages in ftmaterial
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: PR 2594 corrected the ftmaterial/ftcolanim split boundary by moving functions at 0x800BFD04 and 0x800BFD9C from ftmaterial into ftcolanim, renamed their symbols from ftMaterial_* to ftCo_*, updated callers and headers, moved ftmaterial .sdata2 ownership to ftcolanim, and cleaned ftmaterial string/assert usages from address-based externs into source strings and HSD_ASSERTREPORT calls.

key_files.role: Adjusted ftmaterial text end to 0x800BFD04, ftcolanim text start to 0x800BFD04, and moved .sdata2 range 0x804D8C20-0x804D8C28 from ftmaterial to ftcolanim.
Renamed 0x800BFD04 and 0x800BFD9C function symbols from ftMaterial_* to ftCo_* and split ftMObj data size from a larger 0x150 object into ftMObj plus local labels/string objects.

Removed ftCo functions from ftmaterial, removed address-based string/float externs, initialized ftMObj directly, used literal strings in hsdInitClassInfo, and replaced OSReport plus __assert pairs with HSD_ASSERTREPORT.
Added ftCo_800BFD04 and ftCo_800BFD9C bodies, includes needed by those functions, and changed numeric motion-state arguments to float literals.
Declared ftCo_800BFD04 and ftCo_800BFD9C in the ftcolanim header.
Removed declarations for the moved functions from the ftmaterial header.
Updated caller from ftMaterial_800BFD04 to ftCo_800BFD04.
Updated multiple death-animation callers from ftMaterial_800BFD9C to ftCo_800BFD9C.
Updated inline helper caller from ftMaterial_800BFD04 to ftCo_800BFD04.
Updated transformation/common caller from ftMaterial_800BFD04 to ftCo_800BFD04.

what_changed: Split metadata now places ftmaterial .text at 0x800BF260-0x800BFD04 and ftcolanim .text at 0x800BFD04-0x800C0658.
The .sdata2 range 0x804D8C20-0x804D8C28 was removed from ftmaterial and added under ftcolanim.
Symbols ftMaterial_800BFD04 and ftMaterial_800BFD9C were renamed to ftCo_800BFD04 and ftCo_800BFD9C at the same addresses and sizes.
The two moved function bodies were deleted from ftmaterial.c and inserted into ftcolanim.c.
Call sites in fighter.c, ft_0D31.c, ft_0D4D.c, and ftcommon.c were updated to the ftCo_* names.
ftcolanim.h gained declarations for ftCo_800BFD04 and ftCo_800BFD9C; ftmaterial.h dropped the old ftMaterial_* declarations.

ftmaterial.c replaced address-derived extern strings and float constants with direct source constructs such as ftMObj initialization, hsdInitClassInfo string literals, sizeof expressions, and HSD_ASSERTREPORT macro calls.
symbols.txt reduced ftMObj size from 0x150 to 0x50 and introduced labels/string objects for the subsequent data range.
smart_moves: Moved both the split boundary and the function symbols together, then updated headers and all visible callers, avoiding a source/config mismatch.
Kept the moved functions at their existing addresses and sizes while changing ownership and naming, which makes the split correction easy to audit.
Removed source-level dependence on address-named string/float externs in ftmaterial by using direct strings, sizeof, and project assert macros.
Changed ftcolanim include style for ftCo_HammerWait from an angle include path to a local quoted include in the same hunk that added related local dependencies.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a function's subsystem ownership changes, update the split ranges, symbols, owning source file, owning header, and all callers in one coherent patch.

For ordinary assert/report text in decomp source, replacing address-based OSReport plus __assert sequences with HSD_ASSERTREPORT can express the intended source-level operation more cleanly when line numbers and messages are preserved.
Do not keep generated address-named string externs in code when the diff evidence supports ordinary inline strings at the call site.

naming_conventions.name: ftCo_800BFD04 / ftCo_800BFD9C
naming_conventions.convention: Functions moved into ftcolanim and used by common fighter action code were renamed from ftMaterial_* to ftCo_* while retaining address suffixes because semantic names were not recovered in this PR.

naming_conventions.name: ftMObj
naming_conventions.convention: ftMObj remains the canonical global name for the HSD_MObjInfo object at 0x803C6980; the surrounding data was split into local labels/string labels instead of being treated as part of ftMObj.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: The PR moved the ftmaterial/ftcolanim boundary from 0x800BFE6C to 0x800BFD04 so ftCo_800BFD04 and ftCo_800BFD9C belong to ftcolanim.
The PR moved .sdata2 ownership from ftmaterial to ftcolanim and resized ftMObj data metadata from 0x150 to 0x50 with additional labels for following data.
The PR replaced OSReport plus __assert expansions with HSD_ASSERTREPORT at lines 240, 352, and 366 using the preserved report strings.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use direct source constructs instead of address arithmetic
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: hsdInitClassInfo arguments changed from address-derived casts like (char*) &ftMObj + 0xDC and extern string pointers to direct strings and sizeof(HSD_MObjInfo)/sizeof(HSD_MObj).

review_feedback.feedback: Bot report showed 4 new matches: ftCo_800BFD9C, ftCo_800BFD04, ftmaterial .sdata, and ftcolanim .sdata2.

Bot report also listed broken matches under old ftmaterial ownership for ftMaterial_800BFD9C, ftMaterial_800BFD04, ftmaterial .sdata2, and small regressions for ftMaterial_800BF260 and ftMaterial_800BF534; this appears consistent with the ownership move but the PR slice has no human explanation.
No review comments or human review records were present in the loaded slice.

searchable_terms: can't find free color ratio register

notes: Diff evidence is strong for changed files, split/symbol edits, moved functions, header/caller updates, and string/assert cleanup.
PR body is empty, and there are no human review comments, so author rationale beyond the title and diff should not be inferred.
The bot report provides match/regression details, but no local build logs, objdiff commands, or human acceptance rationale are included in the slice.

Some bot-reported broken matches are likely a consequence of symbol ownership moving from ftmaterial to ftcolanim, but that interpretation is not explicitly stated by a reviewer or author in the loaded evidence.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Coordinate split-boundary fixes across config, symbols, source, headers, and callers
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2594 shows a split correction where ftCo_800BFD04 and ftCo_800BFD9C were moved from ftmaterial to ftcolanim by updating splits.txt, symbols.txt, source placement, header declarations, and all visible call sites together.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Prefer source-level assert/report macros over raw expansions when the message and line are preserved
accepted_candidate_records.text: In ftmaterial.c, OSReport plus __assert sequences were replaced by HSD_ASSERTREPORT calls at the same logical assertion sites with the report strings preserved.

source_update_candidates.title: ftcolanim owns ftCo_800BFD04 and ftCo_800BFD9C after split boundary 0x800BFD04
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2594 changed config/GALE01/splits.txt so melee/ft/ftcolanim.c starts at 0x800BFD04 and changed symbols.txt so 0x800BFD04 and 0x800BFD9C are ftCo_800BFD04 and ftCo_800BFD9C rather than ftMaterial_* symbols.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a concrete path/split ownership fact supported by the PR diff, suitable for curator review before promoting into path facts.

source_update_candidates.title: ftmaterial no longer owns .sdata2 range 0x804D8C20-0x804D8C28 in this split
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2594 removed the .sdata2 range 0x804D8C20-0x804D8C28 from melee/ft/ftmaterial.c and added that range to melee/ft/ftcolanim.c in config/GALE01/splits.txt.
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR directly changes split ownership for this data range; curator should decide whether this belongs in path facts.

source_update_candidates.title: Use project assert macros for generated OSReport plus __assert pairs
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2594 provides another example where raw OSReport plus __assert code in ftmaterial.c was cleaned into HSD_ASSERTREPORT with preserved line numbers and messages.
source_update_candidates.reason: This reinforces an existing standard rather than proving a new rule; route to curator only as supporting example material.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all ftMaterial_* functions belong in ftcolanim; the diff only moves the two functions at 0x800BFD04 and 0x800BFD9C.
Do not claim the PR fully improved matching overall: the bot report shows both new matches and broken/regressed items, including matched code -480 bytes.
Do not treat the new lbl_803C69D0 and string labels as semantically named data; the PR uses address-style labels and provides no semantic names.
Do not infer reviewer intent or human approval rationale; no review comments were loaded.
